currentState: Chapter 24 turns the wound public. Vereesa is now the sister forced to read disavowal into the record and then choose not to leave, which means the relationship remains damaged but not severed.
powerDynamic: Institutionally Vereesa speaks for Silvermoon against Sylvanas; emotionally she is already slipping out of that role by staying in the city after the speech. Sylvanas still cannot answer the sister wound directly, but Vereesa no longer accepts institution as the whole truth between them.

physicalDynamics.pattern: The scene stages them first as rider and receiver — blown horse, sealed vellum, arm held level — before letting the family history overtake the formal geometry.
They hold distance rather than embrace. Chapter 19 makes the lack of touch the point.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsVereesa: Uses Vereesa's given name until the confrontation hardens into command and refusal.
nameUsage.vereesaCallsSylvanas: Begins in rank, breaks into sister-language and 'Lady Sun,' then returns to controlled address on departure.

history.event: Chapter 19 introduces the on-page relationship through a recall scene built to hurt twice: institutionally and familially.
Vereesa reports Lor'themar's political exhaustion and asks Sylvanas to come home; Sylvanas answers with the mission instead.
Vereesa leaves carrying Sylvanas's refusal back to Silvermoon.

trajectory: Off-page family bond -> chapter 19's first live collision between blood loyalty and mission loyalty.
